Title: IB Category 1 Workshop
Description: Introductory workshop for educators new to the IB or to a programme, covering IB philosophy and the fundamentals of delivering a subject or role.

Title: IB Category 2 Workshop
Description: Workshop for experienced IB educators that deepens delivery of the four IB programmes and explores teaching, learning and assessment in more depth.

Title: IB Category 3 Workshop
Description: Advanced, specialised workshop that builds on and enhances an educator's professional development portfolio in a focused area of practice.

Title: IB Face-to-Face Workshops
Description: In-person IB workshops hosted at conferences and IB World Schools worldwide, offering immersive collaboration with peers across schools.

Title: IB Online Workshops (4-week)
Description: Primarily asynchronous four-week IB workshops led by an experienced online facilitator, ideal for staff who cannot travel.

Title: IB Virtual Workshops (live, 3-day)
Description: Synchronous three-day virtual workshops that closely resemble the face-to-face experience through a live online platform.

Title: IB In-School / Team Workshops
Description: School-requested workshops delivered to a whole team on-site or online, tailored to ISH's context to build shared understanding across departments.

Title: IB Educator Certificate (IBEC)
Description: University-accredited certificate (PYP/MYP/DP) combining academic study with IB pedagogy through 50+ partner universities to formalise IB teaching expertise.

Title: Instructional Coaching (Impact Cycle)
Description: Research-based, job-embedded coaching using Jim Knight's Impact Cycle to help teachers refine practice through a partnership approach.

Title: Peer Observation & Learning Walks
Description: Structured, low-stakes classroom visits where colleagues observe practice and share feedback, spreading effective strategies across the school.

Title: Professional Learning Communities (PLCs)
Description: Recurring collaborative teacher teams that analyse student data and refine instruction together around shared learning goals.

Title: New Staff Mentoring Programme
Description: Pairs new and early-career staff with an experienced mentor for sustained support with induction, IB practice and settling into the community.

Title: Lesson Study & In-house INSET
Description: Whole-staff training days and collaborative lesson-study cycles where teachers co-plan, observe and refine a research lesson to improve shared pedagogy.

Title: Professional Growth & Goal-Setting Cycle
Description: ISH's annual reflective appraisal cycle where staff set professional goals, gather evidence and review growth against the ISH Community Profile.

Title: Certificate of International School Leadership
Description: Four week-long learning experiences (in person or online) leading to a leadership certificate widely recognised across international schools.

Title: IB Leadership Certificate
Description: University-accredited IB certificate in leadership practice for current and aspiring IB school leaders, building strategic and pedagogical leadership.

Title: NPQ for Headship (NPQH) — International
Provider: Outstanding Leaders Partnership / Best Practice Network
Description: UK DfE-accredited headship qualification adapted for international schools, for current and aspiring heads leading whole-school improvement.

Title: NPQ Senior Leadership (NPQSL) — International
Description: UK DfE-accredited NPQ for current and aspiring senior leaders, developing strategy, culture and team leadership across the school.

Title: ECIS Middle Leaders Certificate
Description: Programme for teacher-leaders, coordinators and assistant principals to build the skills to lead meaningful, student-focused school change.

Title: DEIJ Leadership Development Cohort
Description: Year-long participant-driven cohort deepening leadership in diversity, equity, inclusion and justice across international schools.

Title: ECIS Leadership Conference
Description: Flagship European leadership conference on compassionate, connected leadership for thriving international-school communities.

Title: CIS Global Forum (Admission & Guidance)
Description: Major European forum uniting school counsellors and university admissions to support students' transition to university.

Title: COBIS Annual Conference
Description: Annual London conference for international-school leaders covering leadership, safeguarding, wellbeing, AI and accreditation.

Title: Learning2 Conference
Description: Teacher-led international-schools conference where teacher and student teams design with AI on questions that matter to their school.

Title: Bett UK (EdTech Show)
Description: The world's largest education-technology show, with 600+ exhibitors and free CPD sessions on EdTech, AI and classroom innovation.

Title: Google for Education Events & Educator Groups
Description: Events, Teacher Center webinars and Google Educator Groups for networking and hands-on training with Workspace and Gemini tools.

Title: Google Certified Educator Level 1
Description: Entry-level certification validating ability to integrate Google Workspace and Classroom tools effectively into everyday teaching.

Title: Google Certified Educator Level 2
Description: Advanced certification covering a wider range of Google tools and strategies for technology-rich, student-centred classrooms.

Title: Microsoft Certified Educator (MCE)
Description: Certification mapped to the 21st Century Learning Design framework, validating use of technology to design rich learning experiences.

Title: Apple Teacher
Description: Free self-paced programme earning badges in iPad or Mac plus core apps to design engaging Apple-based lessons.

Title: Common Sense Educator Certification
Description: Free recognition programme building confidence to teach digital citizenship and integrate technology safely and responsibly.

Title: National Geographic Educator Certification
Description: Free certification embedding global issues and an explorer mindset into teaching. NOTE: currently paused for redesign — monitor for relaunch.

Title: Generative AI for Educators (with Gemini)
Description: Free two-hour course co-developed with MIT on using generative AI to plan lessons, create assessments, give feedback and differentiate.

Title: AI for Educators / Microsoft Elevate
Description: Free Microsoft Learn pathway introducing AI tools, Copilot and responsible-AI practice for educators.

Title: AI Deep Dive for Educators
Description: Comprehensive course taking educators from foundational AI concepts through practical, ethical classroom implementation strategies.

Title: AI Basics for K-12 Teachers
Description: Free introductory course on how generative AI works, its impact on education, and how to use it responsibly with students.

Title: ChatGPT Foundations for K-12 Educators
Description: Free practical course, built with OpenAI, on using ChatGPT effectively and responsibly to support teaching, planning and student learning.

Title: Generative AI in Teaching — ISH Staff Sessions
Description: In-house workshops on using generative AI responsibly for planning, feedback and differentiation, aligned to ISH's data and safeguarding policies.

Title: Child Protection Workshops
Description: Internationally focused safeguarding training to build and strengthen child-protection systems in line with accreditation standards.

Title: Safeguarding Training for International Schools
Description: Subscription library of 40+ safeguarding, child-protection and wellbeing courses tailored to the international-school context.

Title: NPQ for SENCOs — International
Description: UK DfE-accredited qualification for SEN coordinators, building expertise in inclusive provision and leading SEN support.

Title: UDL Practitioner Certificate
Description: Harvard GSE certificate equipping teachers to apply Universal Design for Learning so lessons are accessible and challenging for every learner.

Title: Visible Thinking (Project Zero)
Description: Team-based online course on using thinking routines to make student thinking visible and deepen understanding across subjects.

Title: Concept-Based Curriculum & Instruction (CBCI)
Description: Official Erickson & Lanning training in designing concept-based curriculum and instruction that develops conceptual understanding, not just facts.

Title: Introduction to UDL
Description: Course from CAST, the originators of UDL, introducing the framework and Guidelines with an optional credential to evidence inclusive teaching.

Title: Assessment for Learning
Description: University course on formative and diagnostic assessment, including modern analytics, to embed assessment for learning.

Title: Formative Assessment (Dylan Wiliam)
Description: FutureLearn ExpertTrack co-authored by Dylan Wiliam on embedding evidence-based formative assessment, with real-classroom examples.

Title: Canva for the Classroom: 12 Hands-On Activities
Description: Led by Alexandru & Aaron. Work through twelve guided Canva activities and walk away with practical, classroom-ready designs you have made yourself. (Max 30)

Title: Ink & Unwind: Creative Printmaking
Description: Led by Julian Tolhurst. A hands-on creative escape exploring printmaking with unexpected materials — no art experience needed. It's about the process, not a perfect result. (Max 20)

Title: Education for Sustainability: A Whole-School Approach
Description: Led by Wieneke Maris. Explore the sustainability challenges the world faces and education's role, mapping what ISH already does and where we can expand. (Max 20)

Title: DJing 101
Description: Led by Gijs van Vlaardingen. Learn the basics of DJing and musical theory in a hands-on, refreshing session. (Max 10)

Title: Yoga Nidra
Description: Led by Liane Bom. A guided meditation practice that induces deep, conscious relaxation — leave feeling refreshed, calm and at peace. (Max 15)

Title: Rock and Water
Description: Led by Tim Smeets. A physical and mental resilience programme for students — exercises and games that build confidence, respect and self-reflection. Barefoot! (Max 15)

Title: Walk / Run for Fun!
Description: Led by Niels Brouwers. Start the day with some mild, sociable exercise to feel ready and refreshed. (Max 15)

Title: The Secrets of Breath
Description: Led by Karin Holley. Explore breathwork and meditation to reduce stress and anxiety, gain energy and focus, and experience calm. (Max 15)

Title: WWII Walking Tour — On Our Doorstep
Description: Led by Joana Bouza Serrano. A local walking tour exploring the impact of the Nazi invasion right here in our neighbourhood. (Max 15)

Title: Belonging by Design: Wellbeing at Our School
Description: Led by Silvia Semana. Explore the links between belonging, wellbeing and learning, with practical strategies to strengthen belonging in classrooms and across our community. (Max 20)
